disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Greasy yellow scale in a symmetrical sebaceous-area distribution (nasolabial folds, eyebrows, hairline, scalp) that INVOLVES the nasolabial folds — seborrhoeic dermatitis (lupus malar rash spares the nasolabial folds)
trigger_question_examples: Is the greasy scale mainly in the folds beside your nose, eyebrows and hairline (and scalp), and is it symmetrical?
Does it involve the creases beside your nose, or spare them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it's in the folds beside my nose, my eyebrows, hairline and scalp, symmetrically, and it does involve the nose creases.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No facial cellulitis (no unilateral spreading hot deep tender redness, no fever) and no systemic lupus features (no photosensitive malar rash sparing nasolabial folds, no joint pain/systemic symptoms)
trigger_question_examples: Is one cheek becoming hot, deeply red, swollen and painful with a fever (cellulitis)?
Any joint pains, or a rash across the cheeks and nose bridge that gets worse in sunlight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's not hot/swollen on one cheek and I've no fever; no joint pains and it's not a sun-triggered cheek rash.
